Title: IWS - Verify component available in inventory
2. Create a new design.
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to Left Sidebar > Inventory > Tree.

Title: 2. Expand the "Peripherals" or "Sensors" category.
3. Search for "IWS" in the inventory search field.
Preconditions: 1. The IWS component appears in the inventory list.

Title: 2. The component is listed with the correct Q-SYS model number and icon.
IWS - Add component to schematic via drag and drop
2. Create a new design.
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to Left Sidebar > Inventory > Tree.

Title: 2. Locate the IWS component.
3. Drag the IWS component onto Center Workspace > Canvas.
Preconditions: 1. The IWS component node appears on the canvas.

Title: 2. The component shows default name label (e.g., "IWS 1").
3. Input and output pins are visible on the component node.
IWS - Verify default component properties
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
Preconditions: 1. Select the IWS component on Center Workspace > Canvas.

Title: 2. Open Right Sidebar > Properties drawer.
3. Review all default property values.
Preconditions: 1. Component name defaults to "IWS 1" (or next available number).

Title: 2. All property fields display their default values.
3. No validation errors are shown.
IWS - Rename component
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
Preconditions: 1. Select the IWS component on Center Workspace > Canvas.

Title: 2. Open Right Sidebar > Properties drawer.
3. Change the component name to "Conference Room Sensor".
4. Click away to confirm the change.
Preconditions: 1. The component name updates to "Conference Room Sensor" on the canvas.

Title: 2. The name updates in Left Sidebar > Inventory.
3. No validation errors are shown.
IWS - Rename component with special characters
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
Preconditions: 1. Select the IWS component on Center Workspace > Canvas.

Title: 2. Open Right Sidebar > Properties drawer.
3. Attempt to rename the component to "IWS @#$%&*!" (special characters).
4. Click away to confirm.
Preconditions: 1. The component either accepts the name or displays a validation error if special characters are not permitted.

Title: 2. If accepted, the name displays correctly on the canvas and inventory.
IWS - Rename component with maximum length name
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
Preconditions: 1. Select the IWS component on Center Workspace > Canvas.

Title: 2. Open Right Sidebar > Properties drawer.
3. Enter a very long name (e.g., 256 characters).
4. Click away to confirm.
Preconditions: 1. The component either accepts the full name or truncates at the maximum allowed length.

Title: 2. If truncated, no data corruption occurs.
3. The name displays correctly in inventory and canvas.
IWS - Add multiple IWS components
2. Create a new design.
Preconditions: 1. Add a first IWS component to Center Workspace > Canvas.

Title: 2. Add a second IWS component to the canvas.
3. Add a third IWS component to the canvas.
Preconditions: 1. Each component gets a unique default name (e.g., "IWS 1", "IWS 2", "IWS 3").

Title: 2. All three components are listed in Left Sidebar > Inventory.
3. Each component can be independently selected and configured.
IWS - Delete component from design
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
Preconditions: 1. Select the IWS component on Center Workspace > Canvas.

Title: 2. Press the Delete key (or right-click and select "Delete").
3. Confirm deletion if prompted.
Preconditions: 1. The IWS component is removed from the canvas.

Title: 2. The component is removed from Left Sidebar > Inventory.
3. No orphaned references remain in the design.
IWS - Copy and paste component
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas and configure properties.
Preconditions: 1. Select the IWS component on Center Workspace > Canvas.

Title: 2. Press Ctrl+C to copy.
3. Press Ctrl+V to paste.
Preconditions: 1. A duplicate IWS component appears on the canvas.

Title: 2. The duplicate has a unique name (e.g., "IWS 2").
3. The duplicate retains the same property configuration as the original.
IWS - Double-click to open component window
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component on Center Workspace > Canvas.

Title: 2. The window title bar reads "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".
3. Three tabs are visible: "Status", "LED Configuration", "Occupancy Sensor Configuration".
4. The "Status" tab is selected by default, showing sections: Status (with ID button), CPU Temp./Mobo Temp., Network, Sensor Output, Timeout Settings, and Group.
5. The window can be moved, resized, and closed.
IWS - Verify no license required
2. Create a new design with no additional licenses applied.
Preconditions: 1. Add an IWS component to Center Workspace > Canvas.

Title: 2. Review for any license warning or restriction messages.
3. Open Right Sidebar > Properties drawer and review all properties.
Preconditions: 1. The IWS component is added without any license warning.

Title: 2. All component features are accessible without additional license.
3. No "license required" indicators appear anywhere in the UI.
IWS - Verify Sensor Group configuration
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, locate the Group section.
3. Enter a value in the "Sensor Group" field (e.g., "Building A - Floor 2").
4. Click away to confirm.
Preconditions: 1. The Sensor Group field accepts the entered value.

Title: 2. The value is displayed in the Group section.
3. The Sensor Group value can be used to logically group multiple IWS devices.
IWS - Verify Timeout setting in minutes
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, locate the Timeout Settings section.
3. Enter a value in the "Timeout (mins)" field (e.g., 5).
4. Observe the Occupancy State behavior after people leave the sensor field of view.
Preconditions: 1. The Timeout field accepts the entered value in minutes.

Title: 2. After the last person leaves the field of view, the Occupancy State remains true/occupied for approximately the configured timeout duration.
3. After the timeout expires, Occupancy State changes to false/unoccupied.
IWS - Verify Poll Rate setting in seconds
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, locate the Timeout Settings section.
3. Enter a value in the "Poll Rate (secs)" field (e.g., 2).
4. Observe the sensor data update frequency in the Sensor Output section.
Preconditions: 1. The Poll Rate field accepts the entered value in seconds.

Title: 2. Sensor data updates at approximately the configured interval.
3. Changing the poll rate visibly affects the update frequency.
IWS - Verify Status tab Network section displays device info
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, review the Network section fields: Hostname, Model ID, System Uptime, Kernel Ver., Framework Ver.
2. Model ID displays the IWS model identifier.
3. System Uptime displays a time value.
4. Kernel Ver. displays a version string.
5. Framework Ver. displays a version string.
6. All values are populated and plausible.
IWS - Verify ID button triggers device identification
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, locate the "ID" button in the Status section.
3. Click the "ID" button.
4. Observe the physical IWS device.
Preconditions: 1. The physical IWS device performs an identification action (e.g., LED ring flashes a distinct pattern).

Title: 2. The identification action is clearly visible and distinguishable from normal LED operation.
3. The identification stops after a period or when the button is toggled off.
IWS - Verify CPU Temp and Mobo Temp display
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. On the Status tab, locate "CPU Temp." and "Mobo Temp." fields.
3. Observe the displayed values.
Preconditions: 1. CPU Temp. displays a numeric temperature value.

Title: 2. Mobo Temp. displays a numeric temperature value.
3. Both values are within plausible hardware temperature ranges.
4. Values update periodically.
IWS - Verify Bluetooth is not available
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is commissioned and connected on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. Review all tabs: Status, LED Configuration, Occupancy Sensor Configuration.
3. Inspect Right Sidebar > Properties drawer for any Bluetooth-related settings.
4. Review the control pins list for any Bluetooth-related pins.
Preconditions: 1. No Bluetooth configuration options are present in any tab.

Title: 2. No Bluetooth-related properties appear in the Properties drawer.
3. No Bluetooth-related control pins are exposed.
4. The device does not advertise or expose Bluetooth functionality in Q-SYS Designer.
